semanticCore: Slacken, falter, flag, become weak, slow down, or delay

keyDerivations.meaning: To slacken, flag, falter, grow weak, slow down, or lose energy

figurativeOrRareUsages: اذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِآيَاتِي وَلَا تَنِيَا فِي ذِكْرِي (20:42): 'Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not slacken/falter in My remembrance.'
summaryEn: The root represents growing weak or slackening in effort (Wany), commanding Moses and Aaron to maintain continuous, energetic remembrance of God during their mission to Pharaoh.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الفتور والضعف والتقصير والتواني والتراخي في إتمام العمل (وني)، آمراً موسى وهارون بالاجتهاد وعدم الفتور والتراخي في التبليغ والذكر.
rawText: تَوَانَى 6 He was, or became, languid, remiss, weak, feeble, or faint, (T, S, M, MA, Msb, K, *) in actions, and affairs, (T,) in respect of an object of his want, (S,) or in an affair. (MA, Msb.) === تَوَانَى فى الأَمْرِ He flagged, or was remiss, in the affair; (Msb;) i. q. قَصَّرَ. (S.) مِينَآءٌ A port: see an ex. in a verse cited voce شُحُون: it is masc.; its being a substitute for ى: or it is an ancient Egyptian word in origin. وَنَاةٌ A woman languid, or gentle, or grave in deportment, &c.: see أَنَاةٌ. وَنَى فِيهِ 1 He entered upon a thing languidly; and عَنْهُ He passed from it: see عَنْ.
